signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical + Carry
signals.text: Middle East tensions drive safe-haven demand for JPY — USD/JPY retreating from 159.76; safe-haven bid competes with 450bp Fed-BoJ carry differential. Risk-off flow historically unwinds JPY shorts — 158.00 is the first technical level on a sustained risk-off move.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Vol
signals.text: AUD/USD COT LF net long +41,675 (bullish spec positioning) vs retail 88% short (extreme retail short) — institutional vs retail divergence at extreme. Late-trend caution; retail capitulation risk if price extends higher. Watch for LF delta turning positive or AM reducing to shift the read.

signals.title: EUR/USD — CB + Technical
signals.text: ECB 2% vs Fed 3.75% — 175bp EUR carry disadvantage. EUR/USD at 1.1686, testing 1.1700 resistance — 1.1720 is the next level on a continuation. Positioning neutral; no major COT changes.

signals.title: GBP/USD — G8 Outperformance + CB Diverge
signals.text: GBP leads G8 (+0.18% avg, 4 pairs) while BoE 3.75% vs Fed 3.75% — no carry advantage. GBP/USD at 1.3466; 1.3500 is the next resistance on a continuation. Positioning long; no major COT changes.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT + OIS
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short −17,090 (specs short) vs RBNZ OIS hike 100% priced — specs fading a hawkish CB. If RBNZ delivers or signals hike, short squeeze risk is elevated.
